name: Arnold Press
instructions: Sit or stand tall holding dumbbells in front of your shoulders, palms facing you.
Brace your core and keep your ribs stacked over your hips.
Rotate your palms out as you press the weights straight overhead to lockout.
Reverse the rotation as you lower under control, then repeat.
form_points: Press to a full overhead lockout on each rep.
Keep your ribs down so you don't lean back to finish.
Rotate smoothly rather than flinging the weights around.
Control the weights back to the start position.
common_mistakes: Leaning back to muscle the weight overhead.
Rushing the rotation and losing control.
Stopping short of a full lockout.
rom_guideline: Shoulders to a full overhead lockout; elbow ~60° in front to ~170° at the top.

name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Stand tall holding a barbell with an underhand, shoulder-width grip.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides.
Curl the bar up by bending at the elbows until your forearms are near vertical.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your upper arms still and drive the movement from the elbows.
Straighten fully at the bottom for a consistent range each rep.
Squeeze at the top rather than swinging the bar up.
Control the lowering phase instead of letting it drop.
common_mistakes: Rocking the torso to swing the bar up.
Letting the elbows drift forward at the top.
Bouncing out of the bottom without a full stretch.
rom_guideline: Elbow from nearly straight to about 45-50° at the top (front/side view).

name: Barbell Row
instructions: Hinge at your hips until your torso is close to parallel with the floor.
Hold the bar at arm's length with a soft bend in your knees.
Pull the bar toward your lower ribs, leading with your elbows.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Keep a flat, braced back throughout the set.
Drive your elbows back rather than shrugging the bar up.
Pull the bar to about the level of your lower ribs.
Control the way down to a full stretch.
common_mistakes: Standing up out of the hinge as the set goes on.
Heaving with the lower back instead of the arms.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Arm from about straight to a strong pull; elbow ~160° to ~68° side view.

name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie back on a flat bench and grip the bar a little wider than shoulder-width.
Set your feet flat and keep a steady, braced upper back.
Unrack the bar and lower it under control toward your mid-chest.
Press the bar back up to a tall lockout over your chest.
form_points: Lower the bar to touch around your mid-chest for a consistent range.
Keep your elbows tucked at roughly 45° rather than flaring straight out.
Keep your wrists stacked over your elbows through the press.
Control the descent, then press up with intent.
common_mistakes: Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Flaring the elbows wide at the bottom.
Cutting the range short and half-pressing.
rom_guideline: Bar to mid-chest; elbow bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: Bent-Over Row
instructions: Hinge at your hips until your torso is close to parallel with the floor.
Let the weights hang at arm's length with a soft bend in your knees.
Pull the weights toward your lower ribs, leading with your elbows.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Keep a flat, braced back throughout the set.
Drive your elbows back rather than shrugging the weight up.
Pull to about the level of your lower ribs each rep.
Control the way down to a full stretch.
common_mistakes: Standing up out of the hinge as the set goes on.
Yanking with the lower back instead of the arms.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Arm from about straight to a strong pull; elbow ~160° to ~70° side view.

name: Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand tall with a weight in each hand, arms extended at your sides.
Keep your elbows pinned close to your sides.
Curl the weight up by bending at the elbow until your forearm is near vertical.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your upper arms still and let only your forearms move.
Straighten fully at the bottom for a consistent range each rep.
Squeeze the biceps at the top rather than swinging the weight up.
Control the lowering phase instead of dropping the weight.
common_mistakes: Swinging the torso to heave the weight up.
Letting the elbows drift forward at the top.
Cutting the bottom short and missing the full stretch.
rom_guideline: Elbow from nearly straight to about 45-55° at the top (side view).

name: Bird Dog
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Brace your core so your back stays flat and steady.
Reach one arm forward and the opposite leg back until both line up with your torso.
Pause briefly, return under control, then switch sides.
form_points: Extend the leg through a comfortable range until it lines up with your torso.
Keep your hips and shoulders level rather than twisting toward the reaching side.
Move slowly and stay balanced instead of swinging the limbs.
Keep your neck long and your gaze down at the floor.
common_mistakes: Twisting the hips open as the leg extends.
Arching the lower back instead of staying flat.
Rushing so the reach never fully extends.
rom_guideline: Extending leg reaches until it lines up with the torso; hip opens toward ~170°.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand a stride's length in front of a bench and rest your rear foot on it.
Keep most of your weight through the planted front foot.
Lower straight down until your front thigh is about parallel.
Drive up through the front foot, then finish your reps and switch sides.
form_points: Keep your front shin fairly vertical and lower straight down.
Stay tall through the torso with your hips square.
Own a full range and the same depth each rep.
Let the rear leg assist balance, not drive the rep.
common_mistakes: Pushing the front knee far past the toes.
Turning it into a shallow bounce.
Twisting the hips out of square.
rom_guideline: Front thigh to about parallel; front knee ~90° side view.

name: Chin-Up
instructions: Hang from the bar with an underhand grip about shoulder-width apart.
Start from a full hang with your arms straight.
Pull your chest toward the bar, driving your elbows down.
Bring your chin over the bar, then lower under control to a full hang.
form_points: Begin each rep from a full dead hang.
Drive your elbows down toward your sides.
Aim to bring your chin over the bar each rep.
Lower slowly to a full hang rather than dropping.
common_mistakes: Stopping short well below the bar.
Swinging or kipping to finish the rep.
Skipping the full hang between reps.
rom_guideline: Full hang to chin over the bar; elbow ~168° to ~52° front view.

name: Concentration Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench and brace the back of your working arm against your inner thigh.
Let the dumbbell hang with your arm fully extended.
Curl the weight up by bending only at the elbow.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then finish your reps and switch sides.
form_points: Keep the upper arm braced against your thigh so only the forearm moves.
Straighten fully at the bottom for the full range.
Squeeze the biceps at the top of each rep.
Control the lowering phase throughout.
common_mistakes: Lifting the elbow off the thigh to cheat the weight up.
Cutting the bottom short and missing the stretch.
Rushing the reps rather than controlling them.
rom_guideline: Elbow from fully straight to about 40-50° at the top (side view).

name: Crunch
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Rest your hands lightly by your head or across your chest.
Curl your shoulder blades up off the floor by contracting your abs.
Lower back down under control and repeat.
form_points: Curl from the abs and peel your shoulder blades off the floor.
Keep your lower back in contact with the floor.
Move with control rather than yanking on your neck.
Exhale as you crunch up.
common_mistakes: Pulling on the head and neck to lift up.
Bouncing off the floor instead of controlling each rep.
Barely lifting the shoulders off the ground.
rom_guideline: Shoulder blades lift clear of the floor as the trunk flexes; hip angle closes toward ~70°.

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on your back with your arms reaching up and your knees stacked over your hips.
Press your lower back gently into the floor and brace your core.
Lower one leg toward the floor while keeping your back flat.
Return under control, then work the other side.
form_points: Lower the leg through a comfortable range while keeping your back flat.
Move slowly and keep the motion controlled.
Keep your ribs down rather than letting your back arch up.
Breathe steadily as you extend and return.
common_mistakes: Letting the lower back arch off the floor.
Dropping the leg quickly instead of controlling it.
Holding your breath through the reps.
rom_guideline: Leg lowers toward the floor while the back stays flat; hip opens toward ~150°.

name: Deadlift
instructions: Set your mid-foot under the bar with shins close and feet hip-width.
Hinge down, grip the bar, and set a flat back with your chest up.
Take the slack out, then drive your feet into the floor to stand tall.
Lock out with hips and knees straight, then hinge the bar back down under control.
form_points: Keep the bar close to your body through the whole pull.
Brace your core and hold a flat back off the floor.
Push the floor away rather than yanking with your back.
Finish tall, then control the bar back down.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips shoot up before the bar moves.
Rounding the back under the load.
Letting the bar drift out in front of the shins.
rom_guideline: Bar from floor to a tall standing lockout; hips travel from ~90° to straight.

name: Decline Push-Up
instructions: Set your hands on the floor a little wider than shoulder-width.
Rest your feet on a bench or box so your body forms a straight decline.
Brace your core and lower your chest toward the floor under control.
Press back up to a tall lockout, keeping your hips level.
form_points: Hold a straight line from your shoulders to your feet on the bench.
Lower until your elbows bend to about 90° for a full range.
Guide your elbows back rather than flaring them wide.
Control the tempo down and up.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips pike up toward the ceiling.
Short reps that skip the bottom.
Rushing the descent.
rom_guideline: Chest toward the floor; elbow bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: Diamond Push-Up
instructions: Set your hands close together under your chest so your index fingers and thumbs form a diamond.
Brace your core and hold a straight line from your shoulders to your ankles.
Lower your chest toward your hands under control.
Press back up to a tall lockout, keeping your elbows tracking close.
form_points: Keep your elbows tucked close to your body as you lower.
Hold a straight line from your shoulders through your hips to your ankles.
Lower with control until your chest is near your hands.
Press up with intent to a full lockout.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips sag or pike out of line.
Flaring the elbows wide instead of keeping them close.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Chest toward your hands; elbow bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: Dip
instructions: Grip parallel bars and press up to a tall lockout with your arms straight.
Keep your core braced and your shoulders down away from your ears.
Lower under control until your elbows reach about 90°.
Press back up to a tall lockout, then repeat.
form_points: Lower until your elbows bend to about 90° for a full range.
Keep your shoulders down and your torso steady as you lower.
Control the descent rather than dropping into the bottom.
Press up to a tall lockout on each rep.
common_mistakes: Short reps that skip the bottom range.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Dropping fast and bouncing out of the bottom.
rom_guideline: Elbows to about 90° at the bottom; tall lockout at the top (side view).

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie back on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level.
Set your feet flat and keep a steady, braced upper back.
Press the dumbbells up over your chest until your arms are tall.
Lower them under control until your elbows reach about 90°, then repeat.
form_points: Lower until your elbows bend to about 90° for a full range.
Keep your elbows tucked at roughly 45° rather than flaring straight out.
Press the dumbbells over your chest and stack your wrists.
Control the descent, then press up with intent.
common_mistakes: Cutting the range short at the top.
Flaring the elbows wide at the bottom.
Letting the dumbbells drift out of control.
rom_guideline: Elbows to about 90° at the bottom; arms tall at the top (side view).

name: Dumbbell Chest Press
instructions: Lie back on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Start with the dumbbells pressed tall over your chest.
Lower them under control out to the sides until your elbows reach about 90°.
Press back up over your chest to a tall finish, then repeat.
form_points: Lower until your elbows bend to about 90° for a full range.
Keep your elbows tucked at roughly 45° rather than flaring straight out.
Press the dumbbells over your chest and stack your wrists.
Control the descent, then press up with intent.
common_mistakes: Cutting the range short at the top.
Flaring the elbows wide at the bottom.
Letting the dumbbells wobble out of control.
rom_guideline: Elbows to about 90° at the bottom; arms tall at the top (side view).

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with dumbbells racked at your shoulders, palms facing forward.
Brace your core and keep your ribs stacked over your hips.
Press the weights straight overhead until your arms are locked out.
Lower under control back to your shoulders, then repeat.
form_points: Press to a full overhead lockout each rep.
Keep your ribs down and your back supported rather than arching.
Move at a steady pace up and down.
Finish with the weights balanced over your shoulders.
common_mistakes: Arching the back to push the weight up.
Cutting the lockout short at the top.
Letting the weights drop on the way down.
rom_guideline: Shoulders to a full overhead lockout; elbow ~78° racked to ~170° at the top.

name: Forward Lunge
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Step one foot forward and lower until your front thigh is about parallel.
Keep your torso upright and your front knee over your foot.
Push back to standing, then alternate legs.
form_points: Take a full stride so both knees bend to about 90°.
Keep your chest tall and your weight balanced.
Push back off the front foot with control.
Match your range side to side.
common_mistakes: Letting the front knee drift far past the toes.
Falling forward instead of stepping under control.
Shallow, rushed reps.
rom_guideline: Front thigh to about parallel; front knee ~90° side view.

name: Front Plank
instructions: Set your forearms on the floor under your shoulders.
Extend your legs back and rise onto your toes.
Form one straight line from your shoulders through your hips to your ankles.
Brace your core and hold the position for time.
form_points: Hold a straight line — hips level with your shoulders, not sagging or piked.
Squeeze your glutes and brace your midsection.
Keep your neck long and your gaze just ahead of your hands.
Breathe steadily rather than holding your breath.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips sag toward the floor.
Piking the hips up to make it easier.
Dropping the head or shrugging the shoulders.
rom_guideline: Hold a straight shoulder-hip-ankle line (roughly 160-180°) for the target time.

name: Front Raise
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand resting in front of your thighs.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and your torso steady.
Raise your arms straight in front of you to about shoulder height.
Lower under control back to the start, then repeat.
form_points: Lift to about shoulder height rather than swinging higher.
Keep your torso still so the front delts do the work.
Move at a steady pace both up and down.
Own a consistent range on every rep.
common_mistakes: Rocking the torso to swing the weight up.
Lifting well past shoulder height.
Letting the weight drop quickly on the way down.
rom_guideline: Arms to about shoulder height in front; raise from ~12° at rest to ~88° at the top.

name: Front Squat
instructions: Rack the bar across the front of your shoulders with high elbows.
Set your feet about shoulder-width, toes slightly turned out.
Brace and squat straight down, keeping your elbows and chest up.
Reach about parallel, then drive up to a tall lockout.
form_points: Keep your elbows high so the bar stays supported on your shoulders.
Sit straight down with an upright torso rather than hinging forward.
Track your knees out over your toes through the full rep.
Match the same depth on every rep.
common_mistakes: Elbows dropping and the chest folding forward.
Heels rising as the torso pitches over.
Cutting depth short under heavier loads.
rom_guideline: Thighs to about parallel with an upright torso; knee ~85-95° side view.

name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, hip-width apart.
Push through your heels to lift your hips toward a straight line.
Squeeze your glutes at the top with your ribs down.
Lower your hips under control and repeat.
form_points: Reach a straight line from shoulders to knees at the top.
Drive through your heels and squeeze your glutes.
Keep your ribs down instead of arching your lower back.
Control the lowering phase each rep.
common_mistakes: Stopping short of full hip extension.
Overarching the lower back at the top.
Pushing through the toes instead of the heels.
rom_guideline: Hips rise to a straight shoulder-hip-knee line at the top.

name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell against your chest with both hands.
Set your feet a little wider than shoulder-width, toes slightly out.
Sit down between your hips, keeping the weight close and your chest tall.
Reach about parallel, then stand up and squeeze your glutes at the top.
form_points: Keep the weight tight to your chest so your torso stays upright.
Let your elbows travel down between your knees at the bottom.
Track your knees out in line with your toes.
Own a consistent depth on every rep.
common_mistakes: Letting the weight drift away from the body.
Heels lifting off the floor at depth.
Rushing the bottom instead of controlling it.
rom_guideline: Thighs to about parallel; knee bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: Good Morning
instructions: Rest a bar across your upper back with feet about hip-width.
With soft knees, push your hips back and hinge your torso forward.
Keep your back flat and lower until you feel your hamstrings load.
Drive your hips forward to return to standing tall.
form_points: Lead the movement with your hips traveling back.
Keep a flat, braced back throughout.
Only lower as far as you can keep good position.
Stand up by driving the hips, not by rounding up.
common_mistakes: Bending mostly at the knees instead of hinging.
Rounding the back at the bottom.
Chasing depth beyond a controlled range.
rom_guideline: Torso hinges toward parallel-ish; hip angle ~95-110° side view.

name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your body.
Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides.
Curl the weights up while holding the neutral, thumbs-up grip.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Hold the neutral grip throughout so your palms stay facing in.
Keep your upper arms still and move only at the elbow.
Straighten fully at the bottom for a consistent range.
Control the lowering phase on every rep.
common_mistakes: Swinging the body to lift the weight.
Letting the elbows drift forward.
Rushing the reps and cutting the range short.
rom_guideline: Elbow from nearly straight to about 45-55° at the top (side view).

name: Barbell Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against a bench and the bar over your hips.
Plant your feet flat, roughly under your knees when bridged.
Drive through your heels to lift your hips until your body is level.
Squeeze your glutes at the top, then lower under control.
form_points: Finish with your hips fully extended to a flat torso.
Keep your chin tucked and ribs down at the top.
Drive through your heels rather than your toes.
Control the lowering rather than dropping the hips.
common_mistakes: Stopping short of full hip extension.
Overarching the lower back at the top.
Feet set too far forward or back.
rom_guideline: Hips travel from a lowered position to a level, fully extended top.

name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Set a bench to a moderate incline and grip the bar a little wider than your shoulders.
Set your feet flat and keep a steady, braced upper back.
Unrack the bar and lower it under control toward your upper chest.
Press the bar back up to a tall lockout over your upper chest.
form_points: Lower the bar toward your upper chest for a consistent range.
Keep your elbows tucked at roughly 45° rather than flaring straight out.
Keep your wrists stacked over your elbows through the press.
Control the descent, then press up with intent.
common_mistakes: Bouncing the bar off the chest.
Flaring the elbows wide at the bottom.
Half-pressing and cutting the range short.
rom_guideline: Bar to upper chest; elbow bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: Incline Push-Up
instructions: Place your hands on a bench, box, or sturdy surface a little wider than your shoulders.
Walk your feet back so your body forms a straight line on an incline.
Lower your chest toward the surface under control.
Press back up to a tall lockout, keeping your hips level.
form_points: Keep a straight line from your shoulders to your ankles the whole time.
Lower until your chest is close to the surface for a full range.
Guide your elbows back rather than letting them flare wide.
Control the tempo down and up.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips sag toward the surface.
Short reps that skip the bottom.
Rushing the lowering phase.
rom_guideline: Chest toward the surface; elbow bends to roughly 90-100° side view.

name: Inverted Row
instructions: Set a bar at about waist height and lie underneath it.
Grip the bar a little wider than your shoulders with your body in a straight line.
Pull your chest toward the bar, driving your elbows back.
Lower under control until your arms are straight, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your body in a straight line from shoulders to heels.
Drive your elbows back and pull your chest to the bar.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top.
Lower slowly to full arm extension each rep.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips sag out of the straight line.
Short reps that stop well short of the bar.
Rushing the lowering phase.
rom_guideline: Straight arms to chest near the bar; elbow ~168° to ~65° side view.

name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder-width and the kettlebell a step in front.
Hinge to grip the bell, then hike it back between your legs.
Snap your hips forward to float the bell up to about chest height.
Let it swing back down as you hinge, and flow into the next rep.
form_points: Power the swing with a sharp hip snap, not your arms.
Keep a flat, braced back on every hinge.
Let the bell float — your hips do the work.
Stand fully tall at the top of each swing.
common_mistakes: Squatting the swing instead of hinging.
Lifting the bell with the arms and shoulders.
Rounding the back at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Hips hinge to ~90-100° and snap to a tall, fully extended finish.

name: Knee Push-Up
instructions: Kneel down and set your hands a little wider than shoulder-width.
Keep a straight line from your shoulders through your hips to your knees.
Lower your chest toward the floor under control until your elbows reach about 90°.
Press back up to a tall lockout, keeping your hips from sagging.
form_points: Hold a straight line from your shoulders to your knees.
Lower until your elbows bend to about 90° for a full range.
Guide your elbows back rather than letting them flare wide.
Move at a steady, controlled pace.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips drop or push up out of line.
Shallow reps that skip the bottom.
Rushing the descent.
rom_guideline: Chest toward the floor; elbow bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: Lateral Lunge
instructions: Stand tall with feet together and room to step out to the side.
Take a wide step sideways and sit back into that hip.
Keep the trailing leg fairly straight and your chest up.
Push back to the middle, then work the other side.
form_points: Sit back into the bent-leg hip rather than pushing the knee forward.
Keep both feet flat and pointing forward.
Stay tall through the chest as you load the side.
Match the depth and range on both legs.
common_mistakes: Short steps with little hip loading.
Rounding forward over the working leg.
Letting the working knee cave inward.
rom_guideline: Bent knee to roughly 90-110° as you sit into the hip (side/front view).

name: Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and lead with them.
Raise your arms out to the sides until they reach about shoulder height.
Lower under control back to your sides, then repeat.
form_points: Lift out to about shoulder height rather than swinging higher.
Lead with your elbows so the delts do the work.
Keep your torso still instead of rocking the weight up.
Control the lowering phase on every rep.
common_mistakes: Swinging the torso to throw the weight up.
Lifting well past shoulder height and shrugging.
Dropping the weights quickly instead of lowering them.
rom_guideline: Arms to about shoulder height; abduction from ~14° at rest to ~88° at the top.

name: Lying Leg Raise
instructions: Lie on your back with legs straight and hands by your sides or under your hips.
Keeping your legs fairly straight, raise them toward vertical.
Lift until your hips flex and your feet point up.
Lower under control without letting your feet touch the floor, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor.
Raise your legs with control rather than swinging them.
Lower slowly to keep tension on the abs.
Keep your legs fairly straight throughout.
common_mistakes: Arching the lower back off the floor.
Using momentum to swing the legs up.
Dropping the legs down quickly.
rom_guideline: Legs raise from the floor toward vertical; hip angle closes toward ~90°.

name: Mountain Climber
instructions: Start in a high plank with hands under your shoulders.
Drive one knee toward your chest, then switch legs.
Keep alternating knees at a steady pace.
Hold a strong plank line the whole time.
form_points: Keep your hips level with your shoulders as you drive your knees.
Drive each knee well forward for a full range.
Keep a steady rhythm rather than bouncing.
Brace your core throughout.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips pike up high.
Short, choppy knee drives.
Losing the plank line as you speed up.
rom_guideline: Each knee drives from extended toward the chest; hip closes toward ~90°.

name: Overhead Press
instructions: Stand tall with the weight racked at your shoulders and your feet about hip-width.
Brace your core and keep your ribs stacked over your hips.
Press the weight straight overhead until your arms are locked out.
Lower under control back to your shoulders, then repeat.
form_points: Press in a straight line to a full overhead lockout each rep.
Keep your ribs down so you don't lean back to finish the press.
Move at a steady pace up and down rather than heaving the weight.
Finish with the weight balanced over your shoulders.
common_mistakes: Leaning back and turning the press into an incline push.
Cutting the lockout short at the top.
Using leg drive to bounce the weight up.
rom_guideline: Shoulders to a full overhead lockout; elbow ~80° racked to ~170° at the top.

name: Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Hold a weight overhead with your arms extended and elbows close to your head.
Keep your upper arms still and pointing up.
Lower the weight behind your head by bending at the elbows.
Extend back up until your arms are straight, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your elbows pointing up and close to your head throughout.
Lower to a full stretch behind your head for the full range.
Extend fully to a straight-arm lockout at the top.
Control the movement rather than letting the weight fall.
common_mistakes: Letting the elbows flare wide on the way up.
Cutting the lockout short at the top.
Rushing the lowering phase.
rom_guideline: Elbow from a deep bend behind the head to a straight lockout overhead (side view).

name: Pike Push-Up
instructions: Start in a push-up position, then walk your feet in and lift your hips into an inverted V.
Set your hands a little wider than your shoulders with your head between your arms.
Bend your elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor.
Press back up until your arms are straight, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your hips high so the shoulders take the load.
Lower your head under control toward the floor between your hands.
Press back to straight arms on each rep.
Move at a steady pace rather than dropping down.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips drop so it becomes a regular push-up.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
Rushing the descent instead of controlling it.
rom_guideline: Head toward the floor between the hands; elbow ~85° at the bottom to straight at the top.

name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Rest the backs of your upper arms flat on the preacher pad.
Let the weight hang with your arms fully extended.
Curl the weight up by bending at the elbows.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Keep the backs of your arms flat on the pad throughout.
Straighten fully at the bottom for the full range each rep.
Squeeze at the top rather than swinging the weight up.
Control the lowering phase and resist letting it drop.
common_mistakes: Lifting the arms off the pad at the top.
Bouncing out of the bottom without a full stretch.
Rushing the lowering phase.
rom_guideline: Elbow from fully straight to about 40-50° at the top (side view).

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from the bar with an overhand grip a little wider than your shoulders.
Start from a full hang with your arms straight.
Pull your chest toward the bar, driving your elbows down and back.
Bring your chin over the bar, then lower under control to a full hang.
form_points: Begin each rep from a full dead hang.
Lead with your elbows down toward your ribs.
Aim to bring your chin over the bar each rep.
Lower slowly rather than dropping into the hang.
common_mistakes: Short reps that stop well below the bar.
Kipping or swinging to get up.
Not returning to a full hang between reps.
rom_guideline: Full hang to chin over the bar; elbow ~168° to ~55° front view.

name: Renegade Row
instructions: Set up in a high plank with a hand on each dumbbell, feet wide for balance.
Brace your core and keep your hips square to the floor.
Row one dumbbell toward your hip, leading with your elbow.
Lower under control, then row the other side, alternating each rep.
form_points: Keep your hips square and steady rather than rotating.
Set your feet wide for a stable base.
Drive your elbow back and toward your hip on each row.
Lower each dumbbell under control.
common_mistakes: Twisting the hips as you row.
Feet too narrow, making the plank wobble.
Rushing the reps and losing the plank position.
rom_guideline: Arm from about straight to a strong pull; elbow ~165° to ~70° side view.

name: Reverse Lunge
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Step one foot back and lower into a lunge until your front thigh is about parallel.
Keep your torso upright and your front knee tracking over your foot.
Drive through the front foot to return to standing, then alternate.
form_points: Step back far enough that both knees bend to about 90°.
Keep your weight in the front heel and stay tall.
Lower with control rather than dropping the back knee.
Use the same range on each side.
common_mistakes: Short steps that jam the front knee forward.
Leaning the torso far over the front leg.
Rushing the reps and losing balance.
rom_guideline: Front thigh to about parallel; front knee ~90° side view.

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand tall holding the weight in front of your thighs.
With soft knees, push your hips back and let the weight travel down your legs.
Keep your back flat and lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward to stand tall and finish with a glute squeeze.
form_points: Lead with your hips moving back, not your knees bending.
Keep the weight close to your legs the whole way down.
Hold a flat, braced back rather than rounding.
Lower to a comfortable hamstring stretch, usually around mid-shin.
common_mistakes: Turning it into a squat by bending the knees too much.
Rounding the back as the weight drifts away.
Overreaching for depth beyond a controlled stretch.
rom_guideline: Hinge until the torso is roughly parallel-ish; hip angle ~90-100° side view.

name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on your side with your forearm under your shoulder.
Stack your feet and lift your hips off the floor.
Form a straight line from your head through your hips to your feet.
Hold for time, then switch sides.
form_points: Keep your hips lifted and stacked in a straight line.
Support yourself with your forearm directly under your shoulder.
Brace your obliques to hold the position.
Keep your neck neutral and breathe steadily.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips drift down toward the floor.
Rotating the torso forward or back.
Holding your breath through the set.
rom_guideline: Hold a straight head-hip-foot line with hips lifted for the target time.

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Brace one hand and knee on a bench with your back flat and near parallel.
Let the dumbbell hang at arm's length from your working side.
Pull the dumbbell toward your hip, leading with your elbow.
Lower under control to a full stretch, finish your reps, then switch sides.
form_points: Keep a flat, steady back with your hips square through the set.
Drive your elbow back and toward your hip rather than shrugging.
Pull to about hip height for a full contraction.
Lower slowly to a full stretch each rep.
common_mistakes: Twisting the torso to heave the weight up.
Rounding the back out of the braced position.
Short reps that skip the stretch at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Arm from about straight to a strong pull; elbow ~162° to ~68° side view.

name: Sit-up
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat.
Cross your arms over your chest or reach toward your knees.
Curl and sit all the way up until your torso is upright.
Lower back down under control and repeat.
form_points: Curl up segment by segment rather than jerking off the floor.
Keep your feet planted and your chin tucked slightly.
Come to a tall seated position at the top.
Lower with control instead of dropping back.
common_mistakes: Yanking the neck to initiate the movement.
Using momentum to swing up.
Only doing a partial range.
rom_guideline: Torso curls from the floor up to about vertical; hip angle closes toward ~55°.

name: Skull Crusher
instructions: Lie on a bench holding the weight with your arms extended over your chest.
Keep your upper arms pointing up and fairly still.
Lower the weight toward your forehead by bending at the elbows.
Extend back up to a straight-arm lockout, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your upper arms pointing up and still so only the forearms move.
Lower under control to a full stretch.
Extend fully to a straight-arm lockout at the top.
Keep the tempo smooth on both phases.
common_mistakes: Letting the elbows flare wide.
Cutting the lockout short at the top.
Rushing the lowering phase.
rom_guideline: Elbow from a deep bend near the forehead to a straight lockout (side view).

name: Split Squat
instructions: Set up in a staggered stance, one foot forward and one back.
Keep most of your weight through the front foot.
Lower straight down until your front thigh is about parallel.
Drive through the front foot to return to the start, then swap sides.
form_points: Drop straight down rather than drifting your front knee far forward.
Keep your torso tall and stacked over your hips.
Use a full, matched range on both legs.
Control the descent and stay balanced.
common_mistakes: Leaning the torso forward onto the front thigh.
Short, shallow reps that skip the bottom.
Wobbling instead of owning a stable stance.
rom_guideline: Front thigh to about parallel; front knee ~90° side view.

name: Squat
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart, toes turned slightly out.
Brace your core and keep your chest up as you sit your hips back and down.
Lower until your thighs reach about parallel, keeping your heels planted.
Drive through the whole foot to stand back up to a tall finish.
form_points: Track your knees out over your toes rather than letting them collapse inward.
Aim for thighs about parallel to the floor on each rep for a consistent range.
Keep your torso proud and your weight balanced over the middle of your foot.
Control the way down, then drive up with intent.
common_mistakes: Cutting depth short and bouncing at the top.
Letting the knees cave inward under load.
Rounding forward and losing the tall chest position.
rom_guideline: Hips crease to about knee height; knee bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Stand tall with the balls of your feet on the floor or a small step.
Push through the balls of your feet to lift your heels as high as you can.
Pause briefly at the top with a strong calf squeeze.
Lower your heels under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Rise up onto the balls of your feet through a full range each rep.
Pause at the top rather than bouncing.
Lower slowly to feel a stretch at the bottom.
Keep your knees mostly straight so the calves do the work.
common_mistakes: Short, bouncy reps with little range.
Rushing the lowering phase.
Bending the knees to cheat the movement.
rom_guideline: Full heel lift at the top to a controlled stretch at the bottom (side view ankle range).

name: Standing Toe Touch
instructions: Stand tall with your feet about hip-width apart.
Hinge at your hips and reach your hands down toward your toes.
Go only as far as feels comfortable, letting your upper body relax down.
Rise back up with control to a tall stand, then repeat.
form_points: Reach down through a comfortable range rather than forcing the stretch.
Let your hips lead the hinge as your hands travel toward your toes.
Keep the movement smooth and controlled in both directions.
Rise back up steadily to a tall finish.
common_mistakes: Bouncing at the bottom to reach further.
Rushing back up instead of controlling the return.
Rounding sharply rather than hinging from the hips.
rom_guideline: Hinge forward through a comfortable range as the hands travel toward the toes.

name: Push-Up
instructions: Set your hands a little wider than shoulder-width with your body in a straight line.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes so your hips stay level.
Lower your chest toward the floor under control until your elbows reach about 90°.
Press back up to a tall lockout, keeping your body in one line.
form_points: Keep a straight line from your shoulders through your hips to your ankles.
Lower until your elbows bend to about 90° for a consistent range.
Let your elbows travel back at roughly 45° rather than flaring straight out.
Move at a steady, controlled tempo on the way down and up.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips sag or pike out of the body line.
Cutting the range short with shallow reps.
Rushing the descent and bouncing off the bottom.
rom_guideline: Chest toward the floor; elbow bends to roughly 85-95° side view.

name: T-Bar Row
instructions: Straddle the bar and hinge at your hips with a flat back.
Take the handles and let the weight hang at arm's length.
Pull the handles toward your torso, leading with your elbows.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat.
form_points: Keep a flat, braced back throughout the set.
Drive your elbows back rather than shrugging the weight up.
Pull to about the level of your torso each rep.
Control the way down to a full stretch.
common_mistakes: Standing up out of the hinge as the set goes on.
Jerking with the lower back instead of the arms.
Cutting the range short at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Arm from about straight to a strong pull; elbow ~160° to ~70° side view.

name: Bench Tricep Dip
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench and place your hands beside your hips.
Slide your hips off the bench with your legs out in front.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until they reach about 90°.
Press back up until your arms are straight, then repeat.
form_points: Keep your elbows pointing back rather than flaring out to the sides.
Lower until your elbows reach about a right angle for a full range.
Keep your hips close to the bench as you lower.
Press up to a straight-arm finish each rep.
common_mistakes: Short reps that barely bend the elbows.
Letting the elbows flare wide.
Drifting the hips far away from the bench.
rom_guideline: Elbow bends to about 90° at the bottom, straight at the top (side view).

name: Tricep Kickback
instructions: Hinge forward with a flat back and pin your upper arm to your side.
Start with your elbow bent and the weight hanging down.
Extend at the elbow to straighten your arm behind you.
Return under control to the bent-elbow start, then switch sides.
form_points: Keep your upper arm still and level so only the forearm moves.
Extend fully to a straight-arm finish behind you.
Hold a steady flat-back position throughout.
Control the return rather than letting the weight swing.
common_mistakes: Dropping the upper arm instead of keeping it pinned.
Short reps that never reach full extension.
Swinging the weight instead of controlling it.
rom_guideline: Elbow from a bent start to a straight lockout behind you (side view).

name: Walking Lunge
instructions: Stand tall with space ahead of you to move forward.
Step one foot forward into a lunge until the front thigh is about parallel.
Drive up and forward, bringing the back foot through into the next lunge.
Continue walking forward, alternating legs each step.
form_points: Take even, full-length strides so each knee bends to about 90°.
Stay tall and keep each step balanced before pushing off.
Track your front knee over your foot.
Keep a steady rhythm rather than rushing.
common_mistakes: Short strides that push the knee forward.
Losing balance between steps.
Uneven depth from side to side.
rom_guideline: Front thigh to about parallel; front knee ~90° side view.

name: Assisted Pull-Up Machine
instructions: Set the assistance weight and kneel or stand on the platform.
Grab the handles a little wider than shoulder-width.
Pull yourself up until your chin approaches the handles.
Lower yourself back down under control until your arms are long.
form_points: Drive your elbows down and back as you pull.
Keep your body fairly still rather than swinging.
Pull to about chin height for a full, repeatable range.
Lower yourself with control instead of dropping.
common_mistakes: Swinging or kicking to gain momentum.
Stopping short of a full pull.
Dropping quickly on the way down.
rom_guideline: Arms long at the bottom up to chin near the handles; elbow flexes to roughly 58°.

name: Cable Chest Fly
instructions: Set the pulleys and grab a handle in each hand, one foot staggered forward.
Start with your arms open wide and a soft, fixed bend in your elbows.
Bring your hands together in front of your chest in a smooth arc.
Let your arms open back out under control to the starting stretch.
form_points: Sweep your arms in an arc, keeping the elbow angle steady.
Bring your hands together and squeeze your chest at the front.
Stay tall and stable rather than lunging into the movement.
Match the same range on each rep.
common_mistakes: Bending the elbows to press instead of flying.
Letting the handles yank the arms back too far.
Using the whole body to fling the handles together.
rom_guideline: Arms wide in to hands together in front; cross-body angle closes from ~160° to ~105°.

name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
instructions: Set a high pulley and stand facing the stack, hips hinged slightly.
Grab the bar with fairly straight arms out in front of you.
Sweep the bar down toward your thighs, keeping your arms long.
Let the bar rise back up in front of you under control.
form_points: Keep your arms fairly straight and move at the shoulders.
Sweep the bar down to your thighs and feel your lats work.
Keep your torso still rather than bobbing up and down.
Control the bar back up on the same path.
common_mistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into a pushdown.
Using the torso to drive the bar down.
Cutting the sweep short at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Arms forward and high sweeping down toward the thighs; shoulder angle closes from ~125° to ~25°.

name: Single-Arm Cable Row
instructions: Set a low or mid pulley and grab the handle in one hand.
Sit or stand tall with that arm extended toward the stack.
Pull the handle to your side, driving your elbow back past your torso.
Let the handle travel forward under control, then finish and switch hands.
form_points: Lead with your elbow and draw it past your torso.
Keep your torso square and stable rather than twisting for reach.
Pull to about your side for a full, consistent range.
Control the return and match your work on both arms.
common_mistakes: Rotating the torso to yank the handle back.
Shrugging the shoulder up toward the ear.
Uneven range or effort between arms.
rom_guideline: Arm extended to the handle at the side; elbow flexes to roughly 75°.

name: Chest Fly
instructions: Lie back on a bench holding a weight in each hand over your chest.
Keep a soft, fixed bend in your elbows throughout.
Open your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until you feel a stretch.
Bring your hands back together over your chest under control.
form_points: Move at the shoulders in a smooth arc, keeping the elbow angle fixed.
Open only as far as you can control comfortably.
Squeeze your chest as your hands come together at the top.
Keep the same arc on every rep for a consistent range.
common_mistakes: Turning it into a press by bending and straightening the elbows.
Dropping the arms too far and losing control.
Rushing the reps instead of controlling the arc.
rom_guideline: Arms wide at the stretch in to hands together; cross-body angle closes from ~165° to ~105°.

name: Chest Press Machine
instructions: Set the seat so the handles line up with about the middle of your chest.
Sit back against the pad and grab the handles.
Press the handles forward until your arms are nearly long.
Let the handles return toward your chest under control.
form_points: Press smoothly and stop just short of locking the elbows.
Keep your back and shoulders settled against the pad.
Let the handles return to about chest level for a full range.
Control the return rather than letting it snap back.
common_mistakes: Pressing only partway out.
Flaring the shoulders forward off the pad.
Letting the stack pull the handles back too fast.
rom_guideline: Handles at the chest out to near-extended arms; elbow opens to roughly 168°.

name: Face Pull
instructions: Set a cable or band at about face height and grip each end.
Step back to take the slack out and keep your chest up.
Pull the handles toward your face, leading with your elbows out wide.
Return under control to the start, then repeat.
form_points: Lead with your elbows and pull toward your face.
Keep your torso tall rather than leaning back.
Pause briefly at the end of the pull.
Return under control on every rep.
common_mistakes: Leaning back to yank the handles in.
Dropping the elbows so it turns into a row.
Rushing through with a short range.
rom_guideline: Handles drawn toward the face with the elbows leading out wide.

name: Hack Squat
instructions: Set your shoulders under the pads and your feet on the platform.
Release the safeties and brace with your back against the pad.
Bend your knees to lower under control until your thighs are about parallel.
Drive through your whole foot to press back up to a tall finish.
form_points: Keep your back flat against the pad throughout.
Lower until your thighs reach about parallel for a full range.
Track your knees out in line with your feet.
Drive up through your whole foot with control.
common_mistakes: Cutting depth short and only doing partial reps.
Letting the knees cave inward under load.
Coming off the pad at the bottom.
rom_guideline: Thighs to about parallel; knee to roughly 95° side view.

name: Incline Chest Press Machine
instructions: Set the seat so the handles line up with your upper chest.
Sit back against the inclined pad and grab the handles.
Press the handles up and forward until your arms are nearly long.
Let the handles return toward your upper chest under control.
form_points: Press smoothly and stop just short of locking the elbows.
Keep your back and shoulders settled against the pad.
Let the handles return to about upper-chest level for a full range.
Control the return rather than letting it snap back.
common_mistakes: Pressing only partway out.
Lifting the hips or shoulders off the pad.
Letting the stack pull the handles back too fast.
rom_guideline: Handles at the upper chest out to near-extended arms; elbow opens to roughly 168°.

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Set the thigh pad and grab the bar a little wider than shoulder-width.
Sit tall with your chest up and a slight lean back through your torso.
Pull the bar down toward the top of your chest, leading with your elbows.
Let the bar rise back up under control until your arms are long again.
form_points: Drive your elbows down and back rather than just bending them.
Keep your chest tall and avoid swinging your torso for momentum.
Pull to roughly upper-chest height for a full, repeatable range.
Control the bar back up instead of letting it snap your arms straight.
common_mistakes: Heaving with the whole body to move heavier weight.
Stopping short and only doing half the range.
Letting the bar yank the arms straight on the way up.
rom_guideline: Arms long at the top down to the bar near the chest; elbow flexes to roughly 60°.

name: Leg Press
instructions: Set your feet about shoulder-width on the platform and release the safeties.
Keep your back and hips settled against the pad.
Bend your knees to lower the platform under control toward you.
Press back up through your whole foot until your legs are nearly long.
form_points: Lower to a range where your hips stay settled on the pad.
Push through your whole foot rather than just your toes.
Track your knees in line with your feet.
Stop just short of fully locking the knees at the top.
common_mistakes: Bouncing the platform at the bottom.
Letting the hips round up off the pad on deep reps.
Slamming the knees straight and locked at the top.
rom_guideline: Legs near-extended at the top down to a controlled bend; knee to roughly 95°.

name: Pec Deck
instructions: Set the seat so the handles sit at about chest height.
Sit back with your forearms or hands against the pads.
Bring the pads together in front of your chest in a smooth arc.
Let the pads travel back out under control to a comfortable stretch.
form_points: Keep your back against the pad and drive with your chest.
Bring the pads fully together and pause briefly at the front.
Open only as far as you can control comfortably.
Use the same range on every rep.
common_mistakes: Letting the stack pull the arms back too far and fast.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Short reps that never bring the pads together.
rom_guideline: Pads open at the stretch in to together at the front; cross-body angle closes from ~160° to ~105°.

name: Reverse Pec Deck
instructions: Set the seat and handles so your arms start out in front at chest height.
Sit facing the pad with your chest against it and grab the handles.
Sweep your arms out and back in a wide arc, leading with the elbows.
Return your arms to the front under control.
form_points: Lead with your elbows and sweep your arms back in an arc.
Keep your chest against the pad and your torso still.
Open until your arms are roughly in line with your shoulders.
Control the return rather than letting the stack snap the arms in.
common_mistakes: Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Using momentum to fling the arms back.
Cutting the range short at the back.
rom_guideline: Arms forward at the start out to level with the shoulders; cross-body angle opens from ~95° to ~165°.

name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Sit with your feet braced and a slight bend in your knees.
Grab the handle and sit tall with your arms extended in front.
Pull the handle to your midsection, driving your elbows back.
Let the handle travel forward under control until your arms are long.
form_points: Lead with your elbows and draw them past your torso.
Keep your chest up and your torso fairly still through the pull.
Pull to about your belly for a full, consistent range.
Control the return rather than letting the stack pull you forward.
common_mistakes: Rocking the torso back and forth for momentum.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
Cutting the range short at either end.
rom_guideline: Arms extended to the handle at the torso; elbow flexes to roughly 75°.

name: Seated Shoulder Press Machine
instructions: Set the seat so the handles start at about shoulder height.
Sit tall with your back against the pad and grab the handles.
Press the handles up overhead until your arms are nearly long.
Lower the handles back to shoulder height under control.
form_points: Press up smoothly and stop just short of locking the elbows.
Keep your back settled against the pad.
Lower to about shoulder height for a full, repeatable range.
Control the descent rather than dropping the handles.
common_mistakes: Only pressing partway and skipping the top.
Arching the back to push heavier weight.
Letting the handles drop fast on the way down.
rom_guideline: Handles at shoulder height up to near-extended arms; elbow opens to roughly 170°.

name: Shrug
instructions: Stand tall holding the weight at arm's length by your sides or in front.
Keep your arms straight and your chest up.
Lift your shoulders straight up toward your ears as high as you can.
Lower under control back to the start, then repeat.
form_points: Lift the shoulders straight up rather than rolling them.
Keep your arms straight so the traps do the work.
Pause briefly at the top for a strong squeeze.
Lower under control on every rep.
common_mistakes: Rolling the shoulders instead of lifting straight up.
Bending the elbows to help lift the weight.
Rushing the reps with little range.
rom_guideline: Shoulders lifted straight up toward the ears through a short, controlled range.

name: Smith Machine Squat
instructions: Set the bar across your upper back and unrack by rotating it free.
Set your feet about shoulder-width, slightly ahead of the bar path.
Brace and squat down until your thighs are about parallel.
Drive up through your whole foot to a tall finish.
form_points: Lower until your thighs reach about parallel for a full range.
Track your knees out in line with your feet.
Keep your whole foot planted and drive up with control.
Match the same depth on every rep.
common_mistakes: Cutting depth short under heavier loads.
Letting the knees cave inward.
Bouncing out of the bottom.
rom_guideline: Thighs to about parallel; knee to roughly 92° side view.

name: Upright Row
instructions: Stand tall holding the weight at arm's length in front of your thighs.
Keep the weight close to your body as you pull.
Lead with your elbows to draw the weight up toward your upper chest.
Lower under control back to the start, then repeat.
form_points: Lead with the elbows and keep the weight close to your body.
Pull to about upper-chest height on each rep.
Keep your torso tall rather than swinging back.
Lower under control every rep.
common_mistakes: Letting the weight drift away from the body.
Swinging the torso to help the pull.
Rushing the reps with a short range.
rom_guideline: Weight pulled to about upper-chest height with the elbows leading.

name: Ab Wheel Rollout
instructions: Kneel and grip the ab wheel on the floor under your shoulders.
Brace your core hard and set your ribs down toward your hips.
Roll the wheel forward, letting your body extend into a long line.
Roll out only as far as you can stay braced, then pull back to the start.
form_points: Keep your core braced so your hips and ribs move as one unit.
Roll out only to a range where you can keep a flat, stable midsection.
Pull back by drawing the wheel in, not by piking at the hips.
Start from your knees and extend range as your control improves.
common_mistakes: Letting the low back sag as the wheel rolls out.
Rolling out farther than you can control and collapsing.
Piking at the hips instead of pulling with a braced trunk.
rom_guideline: Roll out to a comfortably long, braced position, then return to a tucked start.

name: Ankle Mobility Drill
instructions: Set up in a half-kneeling or split stance facing a wall.
Keep your front heel planted and drive your knee forward toward the wall.
Move through a comfortable range, letting the ankle bend.
Return and repeat, then switch sides.
form_points: Drive the knee forward over the toes through a comfortable range.
Keep your front heel flat on the floor throughout.
Move slowly and control each rep.
Track your knee over the middle of your foot.
common_mistakes: Letting the heel lift as the knee travels forward.
Rushing the reps instead of moving with control.
rom_guideline: Drive the knee forward over the toes with the heel down through a comfortable range.

name: Arm Circles
instructions: Stand tall and reach both arms out to your sides.
Draw small circles with your hands and gradually make them larger.
Move through a comfortable range in one direction.
Reverse the direction and repeat to warm up the shoulders.
form_points: Start small and build to a comfortable range as you warm up.
Keep your torso tall and steady while your arms move.
Move smoothly rather than swinging hard.
Circle in both directions for even coverage.
common_mistakes: Starting with big, fast circles before warming up.
Shrugging the shoulders up toward the ears.
rom_guideline: Circle the arms through a growing, comfortable range in both directions.

name: Assault Bike
instructions: Set the seat height so your knees have a slight bend at the bottom.
Take the moving handles and settle into an upright posture.
Push and pull the handles while driving the pedals.
Hold a steady cadence for the set time or intervals.
form_points: Coordinate the arm push-pull with the leg drive for a smooth rhythm.
Sit tall and keep your core engaged rather than slumping.
Hold a cadence you can sustain for the target effort.
Set seat height so your legs move through a comfortable range.
common_mistakes: Slumping over the handles as fatigue sets in.
Going out too hard and cadence crashing.
Setting the seat so high or low the legs feel cramped.
rom_guideline: Hold a steady cadence for the set time, distance, or intervals.

name: Back Extension
instructions: Set the pad just below your hip crease and anchor your feet.
Cross your arms over your chest or hold a light weight to your chest.
Start bent forward at the hips with a long spine.
Extend up until your torso lines up with your legs, then lower under control.
form_points: Set the pad below the hip crease so you can hinge freely.
Extend up to a straight line rather than arching well past it.
Lead the rise with your glutes and hamstrings.
Lower slowly with a long spine rather than dropping down.
common_mistakes: Cranking into a big backward arch at the top.
Bouncing at the bottom of each rep.
Rounding hard and yanking up with the low back.
rom_guideline: From a forward hinge up to torso in line with the legs, then controlled back down.

name: Battle Ropes
instructions: Grip one rope end in each hand and step back to take up the slack.
Set a shoulder-width stance with a slight knee bend and braced core.
Drive waves down the ropes by moving your arms up and down.
Keep the waves going for the set time or intervals.
form_points: Keep a braced athletic stance with a slight knee bend throughout.
Drive the waves from your shoulders and core, not just the wrists.
Hold a rhythm you can sustain for the target interval.
Keep your torso stable rather than rocking wildly with each wave.
common_mistakes: Standing upright with locked knees and losing power.
Going all-out and stalling before the interval ends.
Only flicking the wrists so the waves die out.
rom_guideline: Drive continuous waves for the set time or intervals from a braced stance.

name: Box Jump
instructions: Stand a short step back from a stable box at a height you can clear comfortably.
Dip into a quarter squat and swing your arms back.
Drive up and forward, landing softly on top of the box in a partial squat.
Stand tall on the box, then step down one foot at a time and reset.
form_points: Pick a box height you can land on softly and in control.
Land quietly in a partial squat with your whole foot on the box.
Use your arm swing to help drive the jump.
Step down rather than jumping down to reset each rep.
common_mistakes: Choosing a box too tall to land on cleanly.
Landing with stiff, straight legs.
Jumping back down and rushing the next rep.
rom_guideline: From a quarter-squat dip to a soft landing on the box, resetting between reps.

name: Burpee
instructions: Start standing, then squat down and place your hands on the floor.
Jump or step your feet back into a plank position.
Lower your chest to the floor, then press back up.
Jump or step your feet back in and stand or hop up to finish the rep.
form_points: Keep your core braced so your hips stay in line during the plank.
Move through a smooth sequence rather than collapsing to the floor.
Land softly on the hop up with a slight knee bend.
Step the feet in and out if hopping feels too much for the set.
common_mistakes: Letting the hips sag when the feet kick back.
Belly-flopping to the floor instead of lowering with control.
Landing stiff-legged on the jump up.
rom_guideline: One full cycle: stand, floor contact, plank, and back to a standing finish.

name: Cable Bicep Curl
instructions: Attach a bar or handle to a low pulley and stand facing the stack.
Hold the attachment with palms up and let your arms hang.
Set your elbows near your sides and brace your torso.
Curl the attachment up toward your shoulders, then lower slowly to full extension.
form_points: Keep your elbows tucked near your sides as fixed pivots.
Curl with a smooth squeeze and pause briefly near the top.
Lower all the way to a straight-arm stretch each rep.
Keep your torso upright rather than swinging to help.
common_mistakes: Swinging the torso to fling the weight up.
Letting the elbows drift forward and out.
Cutting the lowering phase short.
rom_guideline: From arms straight to a full curl toward the shoulders, then controlled back down.

name: Cable Crunch
instructions: Attach a rope to a high pulley and kneel facing the stack.
Hold the rope ends near your head or by your ears.
Set your hips back slightly and brace your core.
Crunch your ribs toward your pelvis, then return slowly to the tall start.
form_points: Drive the movement by curling your ribs toward your hips, not by bending at the hips.
Keep your hips roughly fixed so the abs do the work.
Return slowly to a tall spine rather than letting the stack yank you up.
Hold the rope steady near your head so your arms stay out of it.
common_mistakes: Turning it into a hip hinge instead of a spinal crunch.
Pulling with the arms rather than the abs.
Letting the weight pull you back up too fast.
rom_guideline: From a tall kneeling spine to a rounded crunch, then controlled back up.

name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions: Set a low pulley and stand side-on with the handle in the far hand.
Let the working arm hang across the front of your body.
Set a slight bend in the elbow and brace your torso.
Raise the handle out to about shoulder height, then lower slowly across your body.
form_points: Lead with your elbow and wrist rising together to about shoulder height.
Keep a soft, fixed elbow bend rather than curling the arm.
Lower slowly across the body to keep tension on the side delt.
Stay tall and avoid shrugging the shoulder up toward your ear.
common_mistakes: Swinging the torso to launch the arm up.
Shrugging the trap to lift instead of the shoulder.
Dropping the arm quickly and losing tension.
rom_guideline: From arm hanging across the body up to about shoulder height, then controlled back.

name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Attach a bar or rope to a high pulley and stand facing the stack.
Grip the attachment and pull it down until your elbows sit at your sides.
Set a slight forward lean and brace your core.
Press the attachment down to straight arms, then let it rise back under control.
form_points: Pin your elbows at your sides so only the forearms move.
Press down to full extension and squeeze briefly at the bottom.
Let the weight rise under control to a stretched start.
Keep a stable, slightly forward torso rather than leaning into each rep.
common_mistakes: Letting the elbows flare out and drift up.
Leaning heavily to push the weight with body weight.
Cutting the range short at the top or bottom.
rom_guideline: From elbows bent near 90° down to fully straight arms, then controlled back up.

name: Cable Woodchop
instructions: Set the pulley high and stand side-on with a handle in both hands.
Step out so there is tension and set a shoulder-width stance.
Brace your core and start with arms up toward the pulley.
Pull the handle down and across your body to the opposite hip, then return under control.
form_points: Rotate through your trunk and hips together rather than only yanking with the arms.
Keep your arms fairly straight so the torso drives the motion.
Pivot the back foot as you rotate to let the hips follow.
Control the return so it doesn't snap you back around.
common_mistakes: Bending the elbows and turning it into an arm pull.
Keeping the feet locked so the low back does the twisting.
Rushing the return and losing tension.
rom_guideline: From high on one side down and across to the opposite hip, then controlled back.

name: Cat-Cow
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands under your shoulders and knees under your hips.
Round your back gently toward the ceiling and let your head drop.
Reverse the motion, dropping your belly and lifting your chest and gaze.
Alternate smoothly between the two positions in time with your breath.
form_points: Move through a comfortable range in both directions.
Let each position flow into the next rather than forcing the end range.
Keep the pace slow and matched to your breathing.
Spread the motion across your whole spine, not just one spot.
common_mistakes: Rushing through the range instead of moving smoothly.
Only moving the low back and skipping the upper spine.
rom_guideline: Alternate gentle spinal rounding and arching through a comfortable range.

name: Farmer Carry
instructions: Set a matched weight at each side and stand between them.
Hinge down, grip both handles firmly, and stand up tall.
Set your shoulders back and brace your core.
Walk for the set distance or time with smooth, even steps, then set the weights down.
form_points: Stand tall with shoulders back rather than hunching over the load.
Keep your core braced so your torso stays steady as you walk.
Take controlled, even steps instead of rushing.
Grip firmly and keep the weights from swinging into your legs.
common_mistakes: Rounding the upper back under the load.
Letting the weights sway and knock the legs.
Shuffling too fast and losing an upright posture.
rom_guideline: Carry for the set distance or time keeping a tall, braced torso.

name: Glute Kickback Machine
instructions: Set up on the machine with your torso supported and one foot on the platform.
Line your working hip up with the machine's pivot.
Brace your core and hold the support handles.
Press your working leg back and up to extend the hip, then return under control.
form_points: Drive the movement from the glute by extending the hip.
Squeeze briefly at the top rather than swinging past your range.
Return slowly so the glute stays loaded on the way back.
Keep your torso and low back stable instead of arching to reach.
common_mistakes: Arching the low back to push the leg higher.
Swinging the leg with momentum.
Rushing the return phase.
rom_guideline: From hip flexed at the start to a controlled hip extension, then back down.

name: Hamstring Stretch
instructions: Sit or stand with one leg extended fairly straight.
Hinge from your hips and reach toward the toes of the straight leg.
Go only as far as feels comfortable behind the thigh.
Hold the position, then switch legs.
form_points: Hinge from the hips and reach through a comfortable range.
Keep the working leg fairly straight without locking it hard.
Let your upper body relax down rather than forcing the reach.
Breathe steadily through the hold.
common_mistakes: Bouncing to reach further.
Rounding hard through the back instead of hinging from the hips.
rom_guideline: Hinge forward over a straight leg to a comfortable stretch behind the thigh.

name: Hip Abduction Machine
instructions: Sit in the machine with the outside of your knees against the pads.
Set the starting width so your knees begin close together.
Hold the handles and sit tall against the backrest.
Press your knees outward against the pads, then return slowly to the middle.
form_points: Sit tall and keep your back against the pad through the whole rep.
Push out to a controlled end range rather than forcing it wide.
Resist the return so the outer hips stay working on the way in.
Keep your feet and knees moving together as one unit.
common_mistakes: Leaning forward to throw the weight open.
Letting the pads snap shut on the return.
Using a jerky, fast tempo.
rom_guideline: From knees together to a comfortable spread, then controlled back to center.

name: Hip Adduction Machine
instructions: Sit in the machine with the inside of your knees against the pads.
Set the starting width so your knees begin comfortably apart.
Hold the handles and sit tall against the backrest.
Squeeze your knees together against the pads, then return slowly to the open position.
form_points: Set a start width you can control rather than the widest setting.
Squeeze the knees together with a smooth, even effort.
Resist the pads on the way back out to stay under load.
Keep your torso settled against the backrest throughout.
common_mistakes: Starting from too wide a stretch and snapping shut.
Letting the pads pull your legs open quickly on the return.
Rocking the torso to help move the weight.
rom_guideline: From knees apart to fully squeezed together, then controlled back out.

name: Hip Flexor Stretch
instructions: Kneel on one knee with the other foot planted flat in front of you.
Keep your torso tall and your hips square to the front.
Gently shift your weight forward until you feel a comfortable stretch at the front of the hip.
Hold, then switch to the other side.
form_points: Shift forward only to a comfortable range at the front of the hip.
Keep your torso tall rather than leaning forward.
Squeeze the glute on the kneeling side to settle into the position.
Breathe steadily and stay relaxed through the hold.
common_mistakes: Leaning the torso forward instead of staying tall.
Letting the front knee drift far past the toes.
rom_guideline: Shift the hips forward to a comfortable stretch at the front of the hip and hold.

name: Jump Rope
instructions: Set the rope length so the handles reach about armpit height when stood on.
Hold the handles at your sides with your elbows close in.
Turn the rope with your wrists and hop just enough to clear it.
Keep a steady rhythm for the set time or rounds.
form_points: Turn the rope with your wrists rather than big arm circles.
Hop just high enough to clear the rope and stay light on your feet.
Land softly through the balls of your feet.
Keep a relaxed, upright posture and a steady rhythm.
common_mistakes: Jumping much higher than needed and tiring quickly.
Swinging from the shoulders instead of the wrists.
Landing heavily flat-footed.
rom_guideline: Maintain a steady skipping rhythm for the set time or rounds.

name: Kettlebell Turkish Get-Up
instructions: Lie on your back and press the kettlebell up with one arm locked out.
Bend the same-side knee and roll onto your opposite forearm, then hand.
Bridge your hips up, sweep your straight leg through, and come to a kneel.
Stand up keeping the bell locked overhead, then reverse each step to lie back down.
form_points: Keep your eyes on the bell and the arm locked out through the early steps.
Move through one position at a time rather than rushing the sequence.
Keep the loaded shoulder packed and stable at every stage.
Reverse the steps in order to return under control.
common_mistakes: Bending the loaded arm and letting the bell drift.
Rushing between positions and losing the stacked shoulder.
Skipping steps in the sequence.
rom_guideline: Move from lying to standing and back through each get-up position, one side at a time.

name: Leg Curl
instructions: Set up seated or lying so the roller pad sits just above your heels.
Line your knees up with the machine's pivot and settle into the pads.
Grip the handles and brace your torso to stay anchored.
Curl your heels toward your glutes, then return slowly to the start.
form_points: Match the pad position to your leg length so the pivot tracks your knee.
Curl with a smooth squeeze rather than a jerk off the bottom.
Control the return so the hamstrings stay loaded on the way back.
Keep your hips pressed down instead of arching up off the bench.
common_mistakes: Bouncing the weight out of the bottom position.
Letting the hips lift to help pull the weight.
Rushing the lowering phase.
rom_guideline: From near-straight legs to a full heel-toward-glute curl, then controlled back.

name: Leg Extension
instructions: Sit in the machine with your back against the pad and knees lined up with the pivot point.
Tuck your shins behind the roller pad so it rests just above your ankles.
Grip the side handles and brace your torso against the seat.
Extend your knees to straighten your legs, then lower under control back to the start.
form_points: Line the machine pivot up with your knee joint before you start.
Extend smoothly and pause briefly at the top rather than snapping into lockout.
Lower with control instead of letting the weight drop back down.
Keep your hips and back settled into the pad throughout.
common_mistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight up.
Slamming the stack down on the lowering phase.
Sliding the hips off the seat to gain leverage.
rom_guideline: From about 90° of knee bend to near-full extension, then back down.

name: Leg Swings
instructions: Stand next to a wall or support and hold on for balance.
Swing one leg forward and back through a comfortable range.
Keep your torso tall and let the leg move freely.
Repeat for reps, then switch to the other leg.
form_points: Swing through a comfortable range that grows as you warm up.
Keep your torso tall and steady while the leg moves.
Let the swing stay smooth rather than forcing height.
Hold a support so you can stay balanced.
common_mistakes: Swinging hard before the hips are warm.
Leaning the torso far to counter the swing.
rom_guideline: Swing the leg through a growing, comfortable range while the torso stays tall.

name: Medicine Ball Slam
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart holding a slam ball.
Reach the ball overhead as you extend your body tall.
Slam the ball down in front of you, hinging at the hips.
Pick the ball back up and reset for the next rep.
form_points: Reach tall overhead, then drive the slam with your core and hips.
Hinge at the hips as you bring the ball down rather than only rounding.
Follow the ball down with your eyes and stay balanced.
Reset your stance and grip before the next rep.
common_mistakes: Slamming with the arms only and skipping the hip drive.
Rounding hard at the low back instead of hinging.
Rushing to grab the ball and losing balance.
rom_guideline: From a tall overhead reach to a full downward slam, resetting between reps.

name: Overhead Carry
instructions: Press one or two weights overhead to a locked, stable position.
Set your ribs down and brace your core under the load.
Keep the weight stacked over your shoulder and midfoot.
Walk for the set distance or time with steady steps, then lower under control.
form_points: Keep the weight stacked over your shoulder rather than drifting forward.
Brace your core and keep your ribs down instead of arching the low back.
Take smooth steps and keep the overhead arm steady.
Lower the weight with control once the carry is done.
common_mistakes: Letting the low back arch to hold the weight up.
Allowing the weight to drift out of a stacked position.
Losing balance by walking too quickly.
rom_guideline: Carry overhead for the set distance or time keeping the arm stacked and torso braced.

name: Rowing Machine
instructions: Strap your feet in and take the handle with an overhand grip.
Start at the catch with shins vertical and arms reaching forward.
Drive with your legs, then swing the torso back and pull the handle to your ribs.
Reverse the order — arms, torso, then legs — to return to the catch.
form_points: Sequence the drive legs, then back, then arms for a smooth stroke.
Return in reverse order so the recovery stays relaxed and even.
Keep a steady stroke rate rather than rushing the slide forward.
Finish each stroke by drawing the handle to your lower ribs.
common_mistakes: Yanking with the arms before the legs drive.
Rounding the back hard at the finish.
Rushing the recovery back to the catch.
rom_guideline: Repeat full strokes at a steady stroke rate for the set time or distance.

name: Seated Calf Raise
instructions: Sit in the machine and place the balls of your feet on the platform.
Rest the thigh pad snugly across your lower thighs.
Release the safety catch and let your heels drop for a stretch.
Press up onto the balls of your feet, then lower under control.
form_points: Let the heels drop for a full stretch at the bottom of each rep.
Press all the way up onto the balls of your feet at the top.
Move at a steady tempo rather than bouncing off the stretch.
Keep the balls of your feet planted so your foot doesn't roll outward.
common_mistakes: Cutting the range short at the top or bottom.
Bouncing quickly through partial reps.
Letting the feet roll to the outside edge.
rom_guideline: From a full heel-dropped stretch up to a tall press on the balls of the feet.

name: Band Shoulder Pass-Through
instructions: Hold a band with a wide grip in front of your hips.
Keep your arms fairly straight and raise the band overhead.
Continue the arc behind you as far as feels comfortable.
Reverse the path smoothly back to the front and repeat.
form_points: Move through a comfortable range and widen your grip if the arc feels tight.
Keep your arms fairly straight throughout the pass.
Move smoothly rather than forcing the band behind you.
Keep your ribs down and your torso tall.
