common_mistakes: Gripping too narrow and straining at the back of the arc.
Arching the back to force the band around.
rom_guideline: Pass the band overhead and behind through a comfortable range with straight arms.

name: Sled Pull
instructions: Attach a strap or handles to the sled and take up the slack.
Set your stance and brace your core against the load.
Pull the sled toward you with your back and arms, or walk it backward.
Keep working for the set distance or time with steady effort.
form_points: Drive through your legs while pulling with your back and arms together.
Keep your core braced so the torso stays steady under tension.
Take controlled steps and keep tension on the strap.
Keep your chest up rather than rounding hard against the load.
common_mistakes: Rounding the back and yanking with the arms alone.
Losing tension by letting the strap go slack.
Rushing and stumbling on the footing.
rom_guideline: Pull or drag for the set distance or time keeping a braced, upright torso.

name: Sled Push
instructions: Load the sled and grip the high or low handles.
Set a strong forward lean with arms extended and core braced.
Drive one leg back into the floor and step forward.
Push for the set distance or time with powerful, driving steps.
form_points: Set a forward lean and keep your arms extended so your legs drive the sled.
Push through the whole foot with each driving step.
Keep your core braced and your torso in a straight line.
Aim for steady, powerful steps over the set distance.
common_mistakes: Standing too upright and losing driving leverage.
Taking tiny choppy steps instead of full leg drives.
Letting the hips sag or the back round.
rom_guideline: Push for the set distance or time with a braced torso and driving leg steps.

name: Stair Climber
instructions: Step on and set a pace you can hold with an upright posture.
Rest your hands lightly on the rails for balance only.
Step through the full height of each stair.
Hold a steady cadence for the set time or intervals.
form_points: Stand tall and use the rails only for light balance.
Take full steps and drive through the whole foot.
Keep a cadence you can sustain rather than sprinting the steps.
Let the glutes and quads drive each step up.
common_mistakes: Leaning heavily on the rails to offload the legs.
Taking tiny partial steps.
Hunching forward over the console.
rom_guideline: Step through the full stair height at a steady cadence for the set time.

name: Stationary Bike
instructions: Set the seat height so your knee has a slight bend at the bottom of the stroke.
Adjust the handlebars to a comfortable reach.
Set a resistance level you can pedal smoothly against.
Hold a steady cadence for the set time or intervals.
form_points: Set seat height so your knee stays slightly bent at the bottom.
Pedal in smooth, even circles rather than mashing straight down.
Keep a relaxed grip and an upright, steady torso.
Choose resistance and cadence you can hold for the target effort.
common_mistakes: Setting the seat too low and cramping the knees.
Rocking the hips side to side under high resistance.
Gripping the bars tightly and tensing the upper body.
rom_guideline: Hold a steady cadence and resistance for the set time or intervals.

name: Suitcase Carry
instructions: Set one weight beside you and stand next to it.
Hinge down, grip the handle, and stand up tall.
Brace your core and level your shoulders and hips.
Walk for the set distance or time resisting the lean, then switch sides.
form_points: Keep your shoulders and hips level rather than leaning toward the weight.
Brace hard on the side opposite the load to stay upright.
Take smooth, even steps and keep your ribs stacked over your hips.
Carry each side for a matched distance or time.
common_mistakes: Leaning sideways toward the loaded hand.
Letting the shoulder hike up under the weight.
Rushing and tipping off balance.
rom_guideline: Carry for the set distance or time keeping shoulders and hips level.

name: Thoracic Rotation
instructions: Set up on all fours or lie on your side with your arms out in front.
Place one hand behind your head or reach it across your body.
Rotate your upper back to open your chest toward the ceiling.
Return under control and repeat, then switch sides.
form_points: Rotate through your upper back to a comfortable range.
Keep your hips steady so the motion comes from your torso.
Follow your moving hand with your eyes to encourage the rotation.
Move smoothly rather than snapping into the turn.
common_mistakes: Rotating from the hips instead of the upper back.
Forcing the end range instead of moving smoothly.
rom_guideline: Rotate the upper back open through a comfortable range while the hips stay steady.

name: Treadmill Run
instructions: Clip the safety key and start the belt at an easy walking speed.
Bring the pace up gradually to your target running speed.
Run tall with a relaxed upper body and steady arm swing.
Hold your pace for the set time or distance, then cool down at a walk.
form_points: Run tall with a relaxed torso and easy arm swing.
Aim for a steady cadence rather than long, heavy strides.
Land with your feet under you instead of reaching far ahead.
Ease into and out of the run with a warm-up and cool-down.
common_mistakes: Holding the rails and altering your natural stride.
Starting too fast and fading early.
Overstriding and landing heavily.
rom_guideline: Hold a steady running cadence for the set time or distance.

name: World's Greatest Stretch
instructions: Step into a long forward lunge with your front foot flat.
Place both hands on the floor inside your front foot.
Rotate your inside arm up toward the ceiling, opening through your chest.
Return your hand to the floor, step back, and repeat on the other side.
form_points: Open through your upper back and reach through a comfortable range.
Keep your front foot flat as you settle into the lunge.
Move smoothly between the reach-down and the rotation.
Warm up gradually rather than forcing the end position.
common_mistakes: Rushing the rotation instead of controlling it.
Letting the front heel lift off the floor.
Skipping the reach and only holding the lunge.
rom_guideline: Settle into a lunge and rotate the torso open through a comfortable range.

name: Yoke Carry
instructions: Step under the yoke and set the crossbar across your upper back.
Grip the frame, brace your whole torso, and stand it up.
Set your shoulders back and take a stable stance.
Walk for the set distance or time with short, controlled steps, then set it down.
form_points: Brace your whole torso before you lift the yoke off the pins.
Take short, controlled steps to keep the load stable.
Keep your chest up and shoulders back under the crossbar.
Set it down under control once you finish the distance.
common_mistakes: Taking long strides that let the yoke sway.
Rounding forward under the crossbar.
Losing the brace and letting the load bounce.
rom_guideline: Carry for the set distance or time with short, controlled steps and a braced torso.
